research_topic: Generative agent architectures (2023-2026) for always-on personal novel simulation

source_cards.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
source_cards.authors: Joon Sung Park, Joseph C. O'Brien, Carrie J. Cai, Meredith Ringel Morris, Percy Liang, Michael S. Bernstein
source_cards.key_claim: Memory stream + reflection + planning architecture produces believable human behavior in 25-agent Smallville sandbox over 2 sim-days; ablations show all three components are necessary for believability.
source_cards.relevance: Foundational reference architecture. Defines memory_stream, recency/importance/relevance retrieval, hierarchical planning, and reflection-trees that became the de-facto pattern.

source_cards.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior (ACM Full Text)
source_cards.key_claim: Agents wake, plan, converse, reflect; 25 agents lived in Smallville for 2 sim-days; Valentine party emerged as multi-day social-coordination evaluation.
source_cards.relevance: Source-of-truth canonical formulation. Limits surfaced: hallucination, memory-retrieval failures, formal-LLM tone bleed, fragile importance scoring outside everyday domains.

source_cards.title: joonspk-research/generative_agents
source_cards.key_claim: Official Python implementation of Smallville, Apache 2.0 licensed.
source_cards.relevance: Reference implementation. Pyhton + PyGame, GPT-3.5/4 backed. Useful for prompts, retrieval scoring, reflection tree code.

source_cards.title: Generative Agent Simulations of 1,000 People
source_cards.authors: Joon Sung Park, Carolyn Q. Zou, Aaron Shaw, Benjamin Mako Hill, Michael S. Bernstein, Percy Liang, et al.
source_cards.key_claim: Agents grounded in 2-hour interview transcripts replicate held-out GSS responses at 86% of human test-retest reliability vs. 74% for demographic-only baseline.
source_cards.relevance: Park's own follow-up. Pivots from 'simulated town' to 'simulated individual': interview-grounded agent. Implies our world should ground each character via deep persona doc, not bullet-list traits.

source_cards.title: StanfordHCI/genagents (1000-people repo)
source_cards.key_claim: Public agent bank with restricted-access individualized responses for academic users.
source_cards.relevance: Pattern for persona-anchored agents; useful prompt templates.

source_cards.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
source_cards.authors: Guanzhi Wang et al. (NVIDIA, Caltech, UT Austin, Stanford)
source_cards.key_claim: Auto-curriculum + skill library (executable code) + iterative self-verification yields 3.3x more unique items, 15.3x faster tech-tree progress vs. prior SOTA in Minecraft.
source_cards.relevance: Skill-library is the procedural-memory analogue we want for characters: cooking, swordsmanship, ledger-keeping become reusable callable functions. Iterative-prompt loop is a model for 'agent retries action when feedback shows failure'.

source_cards.title: Voyager Project Page
source_cards.key_claim: Lifelong learning agent with growing JS skill library, GPT-4 prompted black-box.
source_cards.relevance: Demonstrates that without fine-tuning, prompt + code-as-skill scales further than monolithic prompts.

source_cards.title: Project Sid: Many-agent simulations toward AI civilization
source_cards.authors: Altera AI (Robert G. Yang et al.)
source_cards.key_claim: PIANO architecture runs 50-1000+ agents in Minecraft. Emergent specialization, constitution amendment, religion spread (Pastafarianism), cultural-meme transmission across 500-agent run.
source_cards.relevance: Existence proof that >25-agent coherent civilization is achievable in 2024; PIANO splits cognition into ~10 parallel modules vs. Park's serial pipeline.

source_cards.title: altera-al/project-sid
source_cards.key_claim: Tech report + code for PIANO architecture (~10 modules: action, planning, social-awareness, action-awareness, speak, etc.).
source_cards.relevance: Reference for parallel-module agent design with central-decision module that aggregates parallel deliberations.

source_cards.title: A Minecraft town of AI characters made friends, invented jobs, and spread religion (MIT Technology Review)
source_cards.key_claim: Independent reporting on Project Sid emergent behaviors at 500-agent scale; cultural-meme spread documented.
source_cards.relevance: Validates academic claims, including that scale alone unlocks new emergence.

source_cards.title: AgentSociety: Large-Scale Simulation of LLM-Driven Generative Agents
source_cards.key_claim: MQTT-based distributed engine sustains >10,000 agents and 5M agent-environment interactions on cluster; >10^6 agent-steps/hour.
source_cards.relevance: Engineering reference for distributed agent simulation; uses MQTT message bus, cognitive substrate (needs/emotions/motivation). Open source on GitHub.

source_cards.title: tsinghua-fib-lab/AgentSociety
source_cards.key_claim: Distributed simulation runtime open-sourced.
source_cards.relevance: Heaviest production-grade agent-society code base in 2025.

source_cards.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
source_cards.key_claim: Hierarchical OS-like memory management: main context + scratch + recall + archival; tools to swap pages in/out via function calls.
source_cards.relevance: OS-style memory tiering. Best-known production-ish pattern for indefinite conversational agents. Now Letta.

source_cards.title: A-Mem: Agentic Memory for LLM Agents (NeurIPS 2025)
source_cards.authors: Wujiang Xu, Zujie Liang, Kai Mei, Hang Gao, Juntao Tan, Yongfeng Zhang
source_cards.key_claim: Zettelkasten-inspired notes with auto-linking and dynamic re-organization; outperforms vector-only baselines on six foundation models.
source_cards.relevance: More structured than memory_stream: notes have keywords, tags, links forming a knowledge graph that grows over time.

source_cards.title: agiresearch/A-mem
source_cards.key_claim: Reference implementation for A-Mem.
source_cards.relevance: Direct adoption candidate for character-memory.

source_cards.title: Mem0: Building Production-Ready AI Agents with Scalable Long-Term Memory
source_cards.key_claim: Hybrid vector+graph+kv store; +26% LLM-as-judge over OpenAI memory, -91% p95 latency, -90% token cost. Graph variant +2pp accuracy at +1.15s p95.
source_cards.relevance: Production-grade memory stack with concrete cost/latency numbers; library used by 100k+ devs.

source_cards.title: mem0ai/mem0
source_cards.key_claim: Universal memory layer; Kuzu embedded graph + vector DB.
source_cards.relevance: Drop-in for character memory; Kuzu means no separate DB server.

source_cards.title: State of AI Agent Memory 2026 (Mem0)
source_cards.key_claim: Graph memory moved from experimental to production in 2025; recommends hybrid vector+graph for relationship-heavy domains.
source_cards.relevance: Justifies graph-memory for our character-relationship-heavy simulation.

source_cards.title: Memory in the Age of AI Agents (survey, Dec 2025)
source_cards.key_claim: Decomposes agent memory into episodic (events), semantic (de-contextualized facts), procedural (reusable skills); domain-specific weightings: game agents need episodic+procedural; assistants need semantic.
source_cards.relevance: Framework for what memory types our character agents need: episodic (their day) + semantic (world lore) + procedural (their skills/habits).

source_cards.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
source_cards.key_claim: Actor + Evaluator + Self-Reflection trio; verbal RL stored as text in episodic buffer improves task success across coding, decision, reasoning benchmarks.
source_cards.relevance: Reflection loop blueprint. Caveat: same model as actor and judge creates conflict-of-interest.

source_cards.title: Self-Reflection in LLM Agents: Effects on Problem-Solving Performance
source_cards.key_claim: Reflection helps when grounded in external feedback (citations, code execution, search) but degenerates into 'overthinking' when ungrounded; reflection raises first-answer correctness more than self-correction.
source_cards.relevance: Tells us when reflection helps vs. hurts: ground every reflection in external memory or world-state delta, not free-form rumination.

source_cards.title: Reflection Agents (LangChain Blog)
source_cards.key_claim: ReAct, Reflexion, Self-Refine, ToT, CRITIC are stratified by external-feedback dependency; CRITIC + tools is most reliable for production.
source_cards.relevance: Practical taxonomy of reflection patterns + LangGraph implementations.

source_cards.title: QSAF: A Mitigation Framework for Cognitive Degradation in Agentic AI
source_cards.key_claim: Defines 'cognitive degradation' as progressive breakdown of reasoning, memory retrieval, plan coherence due to token overload, planner recursion, memory starvation, context drift; 28% of ReAct paths show latent plan decay leading to goal abandonment.
source_cards.relevance: Empirical evidence that long-running agents drift; gives explicit failure modes to monitor (context-drift, planner-recursion). Argues for a watchdog process.

source_cards.title: CrewAI vs LangGraph vs AutoGen vs OpenAgents (2026)
source_cards.key_claim: LangGraph won enterprise share by 2026 due to graph state, audit trails, persistent checkpoints; CrewAI is fastest to ship for role-based crews; AutoGen is conversational; MetaGPT specialized to SDLC.
source_cards.relevance: Picks: LangGraph for orchestration backbone, CrewAI when narrative needs role-team metaphor, AutoGen if free-form character chat is dominant.

source_cards.title: LangGraph Persistence (LangChain Docs)
source_cards.key_claim: PostgresSaver checkpointer required in production for long-running graphs; survives crashes; supports interrupt/resume; pending-writes preserved per super-step.
source_cards.relevance: Critical primitive for 'tick forever' world: checkpoint each tick to Postgres so process restarts don't lose state.

source_cards.title: OpenAI Swarm (now Agents SDK)
source_cards.key_claim: Stateless handoff-based multi-agent primitive; superseded by OpenAI Agents SDK in 2025 for production.
source_cards.relevance: Lightweight pattern but stateless; not appropriate alone for 'always on' world. Useful for transient sub-agents within ticks.

source_cards.title: a16z-infra/ai-town
source_cards.key_claim: MIT-licensed deployable starter kit for AI town; TypeScript + Convex backend; supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM and llama3+mxbai-embed-large by default.
source_cards.relevance: Closest open scaffold to what we want. TS stack matches our codebase. Convex provides the persistent simulation engine, transactions, scheduled jobs (i.e., ticks).

source_cards.title: ai-town/ARCHITECTURE.md
source_cards.key_claim: Convex shared global state, transactions, simulation engine designed for multi-player; characters configurable in characters.ts.
source_cards.relevance: Concrete reference for tick loop, agent state machine, embedding-based memory recall.

source_cards.title: SillyTavern World Info docs
source_cards.key_claim: Lorebook = keyword-triggered prompt-injection database; bound to character, persona, or chat; can carry behavioral directives, not just lore.
source_cards.relevance: Battle-tested 'world info' pattern from RP community for keeping context lean while injecting only-needed lore. Useful pattern even if we don't adopt SillyTavern proper.

source_cards.title: SillyTavern-MemoryBooks
source_cards.key_claim: Auto-summarizes chats into structured lorebook entries.
source_cards.relevance: Pattern for generating reflection notes that become injectable world-info.

source_cards.title: Thytu/Agentarium
source_cards.key_claim: Open-source framework for AI-agent simulations and emergent environments.
source_cards.relevance: Smaller alternative to AgentSociety; lighter stack.

source_cards.title: Constella: Supporting Storywriters' Interconnected Character Creation through LLM-based Multi-Agents
source_cards.key_claim: Multi-agent system for parallel character development; writer-defined personas converse and surface inter-character tensions.
source_cards.relevance: Closest published academic system to our use case (story-writing aided by agent personas).

source_cards.title: NovelAI V4/V4.5 Multi-Character Workflow (2026)
source_cards.key_claim: Trait isolation per character via separate conditioning streams to prevent token competition.
source_cards.relevance: Production technique for multi-character coherence.

source_cards.title: Can A Society of Generative Agents Simulate Human Behavior and Inform Public Health Policy?
source_cards.key_claim: Generative-agent society used for vaccine-hesitancy simulation; fragile when worldview diverges from training distribution.
source_cards.relevance: Empirical case-study confirming Park's importance-scoring caveat: outside everyday life, the heuristics break.

source_cards.title: Validation is the central challenge for generative social simulation
source_cards.key_claim: Critical review of LLM-based ABMs concludes that validation, not capability, is the bottleneck for trustworthy multi-day simulations.
source_cards.relevance: Directly supports our concern about evaluating long-running coherence. Recommends triangulation: micro-behavior fidelity + macro-pattern reproduction + author-judgment.

source_cards.title: Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5 pricing
source_cards.key_claim: Haiku 4.5 at $1.00/$5.00 per MTok; GPT-4.1 mini at $0.40/$1.60; Batch API gives 50% discount on Anthropic and OpenAI.
source_cards.relevance: Concrete 2026 unit economics for cheap-tier always-on agents. ~10x cheaper than Park 2023 GPT-4 prices.

source_cards.title: LLM API Pricing Comparison 2025 (Intuition Labs)
source_cards.key_claim: LLM API prices dropped roughly 10x in two years; GPT-4 class went from $30/MTok input to $2-3/MTok.
source_cards.relevance: Validates that cost-feasibility for always-on sims is dramatically better in 2026 vs. 2023.
